skill.name: descriptive-action
skill.description: Provides neutral descriptions, summaries, comparisons, and evidence-based analysis without recommendations or action planning.
skill.summary: Creates structured, factual analysis for descriptive requests.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found only ordinary documentation text in SKILL.md. No executable code, cryptography, command execution, network access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found. The static findings are false positives and the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at several SKILL.md lines. The reviewed lines contain the skill name, description, headings, and descriptive workflow text only. No hash function, encryption API, credential handling, or security-sensitive algorithm appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are plain Markdown instructions and metadata. There is no executable cryptographic operation or security-sensitive data flow to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Pattern Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md line 27. The line only instructs the model to avoid normative language. No command, host inspection, environment probing, or system inventory behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a writing-style rule, not a reconnaissance command or procedure. The surrounding context is descriptive analysis guidance.

content.value_statement: Descriptive requests can drift into advice, plans, or unsupported conclusions. This skill keeps responses factual, structured, and clearly separated between evidence and inference.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes provided text, data, events, systems, or artifacts.
Explains concepts and observations without giving recommendations.
Separates directly supported facts from labeled inferences.
Compares inputs using neutral, structured sections.
Reports missing information and open questions that limit confidence.
limitations: Does not provide recommendations unless the user explicitly asks for them.
Does not create step-by-step plans or strategies for action requests.
Depends on the quality and completeness of the supplied material.
Does not verify external facts unless another tool or source is available.

use_cases.title: Summarize Meeting Notes
use_cases.description: Turn long notes into neutral summaries with facts, themes, and open questions.

use_cases.title: Compare Product Feedback
use_cases.description: Describe similarities, differences, patterns, and constraints across user comments.

use_cases.title: Explain Technical Material
use_cases.description: Convert dense documentation or system descriptions into clear, bounded explanations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a short neutral summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the following material. Separate facts from inferences and list any open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Explanation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the input needs organized explanation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this material using sections for summary, details, evidence, and unknowns. Avoid recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Neutral Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when comparing two or more items.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these items by describing shared traits, differences, evidence, and confidence limits. Do not choose a winner.

prompt_templates.title: Evidence-Bounded Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for complex analysis with uncertain inputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the provided material. Label facts, interpretations, trends, constraints, and missing information. Keep conclusions bounded by evidence.

output_examples.input: A user provides meeting notes from a project status call.
output: Summary: The project is progressing, but testing and staffing remain constraints.
Details: Delivery work is active, quality checks are incomplete, and ownership questions remain.
Evidence: The notes mention completed design work, pending test cases, and unresolved staffing decisions.
Open questions: The final launch date and responsible owners are not confirmed.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a comparison of two customer feedback themes.
output: Summary: Both themes describe friction, but they focus on different parts of the experience.
Details: One theme concerns setup difficulty. The other concerns ongoing workflow interruptions.
Evidence: Setup comments mention onboarding and configuration. Workflow comments mention repeated manual steps.
Open questions: The sample size and customer segments are not provided.

output_examples.input: A user provides a system description and asks what it means.
output: Summary: The system processes incoming records, applies validation, and stores accepted results.
Details: Validation appears central because rejected records stop before storage.
Evidence: The description names an input stage, validation rules, and a storage stage.
Open questions: Error handling, retry behavior, and data retention are not described.

best_practices: Provide the source material and define the desired scope before asking for analysis.
Ask for facts, inferences, evidence, and open questions when accuracy matters.
State the audience so the explanation uses the right level of detail.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when you need recommendations or a decision.
Do not ask it to invent facts that are missing from the input.
Do not mix descriptive analysis with action planning in the same request.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It creates neutral summaries, explanations, comparisons, and analyses from provided material.

faq.question: Does it make recommendations?
faq.answer: No. It avoids recommendations unless the user explicitly asks for advice or next steps.

faq.question: Can it analyze data?
faq.answer: Yes. It can describe patterns, constraints, and observations from data supplied in the prompt.

faq.question: How does it handle uncertainty?
faq.answer: It labels inferences, identifies missing information, and lists open questions that limit confidence.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It can describe code, system behavior, documentation, and changes without prescribing edits.

faq.question: Does it access files or external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill is a Markdown instruction file and does not include executable integrations.
